BNS-001.user: I want to file a complaint against my husband.

BNS-001.bot:
Could you describe the specific wrongdoing by your husband that you want to complain about? For example, physical abuse, emotional cruelty, stalking, or any other conduct?  

Has your husband subjected you to any form of cruelty or violence?

BNS-001.user: My husband subjected me to physical abuse and emotional cruelty when he locked me in a room and beat me with a belt.

BNS-001.bot:
Did your husband lock you in a room?  

Did he beat you with a belt in a way that caused you bodily pain or injury?

BNS-001.user:
Yes, my husband locked me in a room. 

Yes, he beat me with a belt in a way that caused me bodily pain and injury.

BNS-001.bot:
Section 85 – Cruelty by Husband  
What it means: A husband who subjects his wife to cruelty—behaviour that causes her to suffer physical or mental harm—can be punished.  
Why it applies to you: Your husband locked you in a room and beat you with a belt, which is a clear act of cruelty.  
Punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and a fine.  
Cognizable?: Cognizable if information relating to the commission of the offence is given to an officer in charge of a police station by the person aggrieved by the offence or by any person related to her by blood, marriage or adoption or if there is no such relative, by any public servant belonging to such class or category as may be notified by the State Government in this behalf.  
Bailable?: Non-bailable.  
Triable By: Magistrate of the first class.  

Section 86 – Definition of Cruelty  
What it means: Provides the legal definition of “cruelty” for the purposes of Section 85, including acts likely to cause grave injury or danger to life, limb or health.  
Why it applies to you: The beating with a belt caused bodily pain and injury, fitting the definition of cruelty.  
Punishment: Not specified.  
Cognizable?: Not specified.  
Bailable?: Not specified.  
Triable By: Not specified.  

Section 114 – Causing Hurt  
What it means: Whoever causes bodily pain, disease or infirmity to another person is said to cause hurt.  
Why it applies to you: The belt beating caused you bodily pain and injury.  
Punishment: Not specified.  
Cognizable?: Not specified.  
Bailable?: Not specified.  
Triable By: Not specified.  

Section 115 – Voluntarily Causing Hurt  
What it means: Whoever intentionally or knowingly causes hurt to another person is guilty of voluntarily causing hurt.  
Why it applies to you: Your husband intentionally beat you with a belt, knowing it would cause hurt.  
Punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year and/or a fine of up to 10,000 rupees.  
Cognizable?: Non-cognizable.  
Bailable?: Bailable.  
Triable By: Any Magistrate.  

Section 74 – Assault or Use of Criminal Force  
What it means: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force against a woman, knowing it may outrage her modesty or cause injury, is punishable.  
Why it applies to you: The belt beating is an assault with criminal force that caused injury.  
Punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and a fine.  
Cognizable?: Cognizable.  
Bailable?: Non-bailable.  
Triable By: Any Magistrate.  

Section 333 – Wrongful Restraint and House‑Trespass  
What it means: Whoever commits house‑trespass or wrongfully restrains a person, or puts them in fear of hurt, is punishable.  
Why it applies to you: Your husband locked you in a room, wrongfully restraining you.  
Punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and a fine.  
Cognizable?: Non-cognizable.  
Bailable?: Non-bailable.  
Triable By: Any Magistrate.

BNS-002.user: Someone tried to kill me with a rod last night.
My neighbor attacked me with a sharp iron rod near my gate around 10 PM last night.

BNS-002.bot:
Did you suffer any injury or hurt as a result of the attack?  
Did the neighbor threaten you with death or serious harm (e.g., saying he would kill you or cause grievous injury)?

BNS-002.user:
Yes, I suffered a fractured bone in my shoulder as a result of the attack. 

My neighbor threatened me with death by saying 'I will finish you today'.

BNS-002.bot:
Section 118 - Voluntary causing grievous hurt with instrument likely to cause death  
What it means: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt with an instrument likely to cause death (like a sharp rod) is punished with imprisonment for life or up to ten years, plus a fine.  
Why it applies to you: You were struck with a sharp iron rod and suffered a fractured shoulder, which is grievous hurt caused by an instrument likely to cause death.  
Punishment: Imprisonment for 3 years, or fine of 20,000 rupees, or both.  
Cognizable?: Cognizable.  
Bailable?: Non‑bailable.  
Triable By: Any Magistrate.  

Section 130 - Assault by threat of force  
What it means: Using force or a gesture that makes a person believe you will use criminal force is an assault.  
Why it applies to you: The neighbor used a sharp rod and threatened you with death, which constitutes assault.  
Punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years, or a fine, or both.  
Cognizable?: Not specified.  
Bailable?: Not specified.  
Triable By: Not specified.  

Section 351 - Criminal intimidation by threat of death or grievous hurt  
What it means: Threatening another person with death or grievous hurt, or with an offence punishable by death or life imprisonment, is criminal intimidation.  
Why it applies to you: The neighbor said, “I will finish you today,” threatening death.  
Punishment: Imprisonment for 2 years, or a fine, or both.  
Cognizable?: Non‑cognizable.  
Bailable?: Bailable.  
Triable By: Any Magistrate.

BNS-003.user: My maid took my jewelry from the house.

BNS-003.bot:
Could you confirm whether the maid took the jewelry without your permission and intended to keep or sell it?  
Also, was the jewelry in your possession at the time it was taken?

BNS-003.user:
Yes, the maid took the jewelry without my permission and intended to keep or sell it. 

Yes, the jewelry was in my possession at the time it was taken.
The house help has been working with us for 3 years.
The house help has been working with us for 3 years.
My house help has been working with us for 3 years.
